module-5-gradebook-project.label: Module Project: Build a Gradebook
module-5-gradebook-project.summary: Build a small gradebook one realistic step at a time: roster first, then scores, a high-score view, and a ranked summary.

sketch0.title: Your first client: a teacher with messy scores

mp-1-create-student-list.title: Create a student list
mp-1-create-student-list.prompt: Ms. Rivera is setting up the first version of her gradebook. Start the project by creating an empty list named `students`, adding `Ava`, `Mia`, and `Zoe` in that order, and printing the finished roster.
mp-1-create-student-list.hint: This first step only builds the roster. Use `append()` for each student, then print `students`.

help.concept: This project grows like a real gradebook: each step starts from a clean working version, then adds one useful feature for the teacher.
help.hint_1: Start with `students = []`, then append the three names in order.
help.hint_2: Finish the first working version by printing `students`.

mp-1-create-student-list.starterCode:
students = []

# Start the gradebook with the class roster.
# Add Ava, Mia, and Zoe in that order, then print students.

mp-2-store-scores.title: Store scores in a dictionary
mp-2-store-scores.prompt: Keep the working roster from the last step. Now grow the gradebook by adding a dictionary named `scores` with `Ava: 92`, `Mia: 85`, and `Zoe: 97`, then print the score dictionary too.
mp-2-store-scores.hint: Leave the roster code in place, then add the score lookup right under it.

help.concept: A list keeps the class roster in order; a dictionary lets the gradebook look up each student’s score by name.
help.hint_2: Print `scores` after the roster so both parts of the gradebook are visible.

mp-2-store-scores.starterCode:
students = []
students.append('Ava')
students.append('Mia')
students.append('Zoe')
print(students)

# Keep the working roster and add the score lookup for these students.
# Create scores for Ava, Mia, and Zoe, then print scores.

mp-3-print-passing-students.title: Print high-scoring students
mp-3-print-passing-students.prompt: Keep the roster and score dictionary you already built. Now add one more view for Ms. Rivera by printing only the students whose score is 90 or higher, one name per line.
mp-3-print-passing-students.hint: Reuse the existing `students` list and `scores` dictionary instead of rebuilding them.

help.concept: This step connects two collections: the list gives the order, and the dictionary gives the score for each name.
help.hint_1: Loop through the existing roster with `for student in students:`.
help.hint_2: Inside the loop, check `scores[student] >= 90` before printing the name.

mp-3-print-passing-students.starterCode:
students = ['Ava', 'Mia', 'Zoe']
scores = {'Ava': 92, 'Mia': 85, 'Zoe': 97}
print(students)
print(scores)

# Keep this gradebook and add the honor-board filter.
# Loop through students in roster order and print names with scores of 90 or higher.

mp-4-sort-grade-summary.title: Sort the grade summary
mp-4-sort-grade-summary.prompt: Start from the passing-students version you just made. Keep the same score data, but replace that filtered view with a ranked summary sorted from highest score to lowest score, printed as `Name: score`.
mp-4-sort-grade-summary.hint: Turn the working score dictionary into a report by sorting its items and formatting each line.

help.concept: A useful report is not just stored data; it presents the data in an order that helps someone make a decision.
help.hint_1: Use `sorted(scores.items(), key=lambda item: item[1], reverse=True)` to rank the score pairs.
help.hint_2: Loop through the sorted pairs and print each line with an f-string like `f"{name}: {score}"`.

try-module-5-gradebook-project-sketch0.title: Preview a gradebook list
try-module-5-gradebook-project-sketch0.prompt: Use `append()` to preview how the gradebook project will grow a student list.

help.hint_1: Write the line for this step, then run the program to see what changes.
help.hint_2: When you run it, print only the value or lines the prompt asks for.

try-module-5-gradebook-project-sketch0.starterCode:
students = ['Ava', 'Mia']

# Add Zoe and print students.

try_module_5_gradebook_project_sketch0.title: Try it yourself
try_module_5_gradebook_project_sketch0.prompt: Use `append()` to preview how the gradebook project will grow a student list.

sketch-1.title: Your first client: a teacher with messy scores
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
Ms. Rivera has student names in one place, scores in another, and no clean way to see who is doing well. In this module project, you will build a small gradebook for her.

The project grows smoothly: each step starts from the previous canonical solution and adds one new feature. By the end, the program will keep a roster, connect names to scores, filter high-scoring students, and print a sorted summary.
